Grid.TextBlock:
The Director of Central Intelligence
Washington D.C., 20505

JCS WASHINGTON DC//J3//
USCINCLANT NORFOLK VA
USCINCEUR VAIHINGEN GE
USCINCPAC HONOLULU HI
CINCSAC OFFUTT AFB NE

TextBlock.Text: Soviet Expansion of Naval Operations into the South Atlantic

Grid.TextBlock:
1. We assess that the Soviets have deliberately opened the southern oceans as active theaters of naval warfare. Liaison reporting and national technical means indicate a Soviet raiding force operating in the South Atlantic. This force unlocated as of 28JUN.
2. The core units of this Raider Group departed Northern Fleet waters prior to outbreak of hostilities, but contact was lost in the central Atlantic during a period of heavy weather.
3. On 26JUN, four U.S.-flagged merchants were attacked east of Tristan da Cunha while transiting to Cape Town. Survivors were rescued by Royal Navy and transferred to Saint Helena. Crew interviews indicate coordinated Soviet air and surface attacks. Some crews were unaware that hostilities had commenced.
4. Reports of attack by light aircraft have raised concern within DoD, suggesting Raider Group could be operating with Soviet naval aviation support. Given extreme distance from any known Soviet bases, we assess the likely use of converted RoRo AGOSTINHO NETO as an improvised aviation platform operating Yak-38 VTOL fighters. This ship was previously trialed for VTOL operations after U.K. experience in Falklands.
5. Ongoing commitments in Europe and the Pacific limit our ability to respond rapidly in this theater. The Republic of South Africa has agreed to make limited air/naval assets available under specific terms. This cooperation has required tacit concessions regarding enforcement of existing sanctions and arms embargoes, with further policy implications requiring senior-level review. Our force alignment in the Atlantic will be forwarded ASAP.

Distribution by ER/28 June 1985 w/Att
Orig - Each Addressee
1 - DCI
1 - DDCI
1 - EXDIR
1 - C/NIC
1 - VC/NIC
1 - NIO/Warning
1 - ER File
